---
title: "XRF_PCA"
output: html_document
date: "2024-04-30"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

rm(list = ls())
setwd("~/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-UniversityofKansas/Bryan-MS_PhD/PHD-PROJECT/PuertoRico/CHAPTER3/XRF/R-Plotting")

```

Stop data displayed in scientific notation
```{r stop scipen}
options(scipen = 999)
```



```{r load packages, warning=FALSE}

library(magick)
library(tiff)
library(readr)
library(signal) 
library(raster)
library(ggplot2)
library(gridExtra)
library(png)
library(grid)
library(ggbiplot)
library(plotly)
library(compositions)
library(ggrepel)
library(tidyr)
library(MASS)
library(zCompositions)
library(dplyr)
```


```{r To reset data without erasing functions}
rm(list = ls()[!sapply(ls(), function(x) is.function(get(x)))])
```


```{r Load data}
#PS-C2
XRF_C2 <- read.csv("input/XRF_data_C2.csv")
# C2 depths
C2_depth <- scan("input/XRF_data_C2_depth.txt", sep = ",", what = numeric(), quiet = TRUE)

#PC-C3
XRF_C3 <- read.csv("input/XRF_data_C3.csv")
# C3 depths
C3_depth <- scan("input/XRF_data_C3_depth.txt", sep = ",", what = numeric(), quiet = TRUE)

```




```{r Check LOQ}
# Estimate approximate LOQ for Sb at 10s exposure
Sb_LOQ_30s <- 1740   # assumed conservative value
Sb_LOQ_10s <- Sb_LOQ_30s * (10/30)

# Check mean counts
mean_counts_Sb <- mean(XRF_C2$Zr, na.rm = TRUE)

# Check if Sb is reliably above LOQ
if(mean_counts_Sb < Sb_LOQ_30s){
  message("Sb is below the LOQ; likely analytical noise.")
} else {
  message("Sb is above the LOQ; check for spectral overlap.")
}

```





#Functions

```{r clr transformation function}
clr_transform <- function(data, selected_elements, depth_data) {
  # Subset the data to include only the selected elements
  subset_data <- data[, selected_elements]
  
  # Ensure depth_data is a vector (if it's not already)
  depth_vec <- depth_data
  
  # Replace zeros with half of the minimum non-zero value in each column
  replaceZeros <- function(x) {
    nonzero <- x[x > 0 & !is.na(x)]
    if (length(nonzero) > 0) {
      x[x == 0] <- min(nonzero) / 2
    }
    return(x)
  }
  subset_data_clean <- as.data.frame(lapply(subset_data, replaceZeros))
  
  # Identify the rows with complete cases (no NA values)
  complete_idx <- complete.cases(subset_data_clean)
  
  # Subset the cleaned data and the depth vector using the same indices
  subset_data_clean <- subset_data_clean[complete_idx, ]
  depth_clean <- depth_vec[complete_idx]
  
  # Compute the geometric mean for each row
  geomMean <- apply(subset_data_clean, 1, function(x) exp(mean(log(x))))
  
  # Compute the clr transformation for each element in each row
  clr_data <- subset_data_clean  # initialize a data frame for clr values
  for (i in 1:nrow(subset_data_clean)) {
    clr_data[i, ] <- log(subset_data_clean[i, ] / geomMean[i])
  }
  
  # Add the depth information to the clr_data dataframe as the first column
  clr_data$Depth <- depth_clean
  clr_data <- clr_data[, c("Depth", setdiff(names(clr_data), "Depth"))]
  
  return(clr_data)
}

```

#clr-transformation for both sediment cores

```{r Clean element list for noise prior to clr-tranformation}

# All elements and set thresholds
elements <- c("Mg", "Al", "Si", "P", "S", "Cl", "Ar", "K", "Ca", "Ti", "V", "Cr", "Mn", "Fe", "Ni", "Cu", "Zn", "Ge", "As", "Br", "Rb", "Sr", "Y", "Zr", "Sb", "Cs", "Ba", "Ce", "Pr", "Nd", "Sm", "Pb") #32 elements

zero_threshold <- 0.80
count_threshold <- 40

# Identify numeric (element) columns only
elements_C2 <- XRF_C2[elements] %>% dplyr::select(where(is.numeric))
elements_C3 <- XRF_C3[elements] %>% dplyr::select(where(is.numeric))


# Calculate stats in one step
stats_C2 <- data.frame(
  element    = names(elements_C2),
  prop_zero  = colMeans(elements_C2 == 0, na.rm=TRUE),
  mean_count = colMeans(elements_C2, na.rm=TRUE)
)

stats_C3 <- data.frame(
  element    = names(elements_C3),
  prop_zero  = colMeans(elements_C3 == 0, na.rm=TRUE),
  mean_count = colMeans(elements_C3, na.rm=TRUE)
)

# Filter based on your thresholds
elements_keep_C2 <- stats_C2 %>%
  filter(prop_zero <= zero_threshold, mean_count >= count_threshold) %>%
  pull(element)

elements_keep_C2 <- elements_keep_C2[!elements_keep_C2 %in% c("Sb", "Zr", "Ge")] #Take out Sb and Zr, as this is an analyitical error


elements_keep_C3 <- stats_C3 %>%
  filter(prop_zero <= zero_threshold, mean_count >= count_threshold) %>%
  pull(element)

elements_keep_C3 <- c(elements_keep_C3, "S", "K") #Include Sulfur and potassium for C3

elements_keep_C3 <- elements_keep_C3[!elements_keep_C3 %in% c("Sb", "Zr", "Ge")]


print("C2 elements to keep:")
print(elements_keep_C2)

print("C3 elements to keep:")
print(elements_keep_C3)

rm(stats_C2)
rm(stats_C3)
rm(elements_C2)
rm(elements_C3)

```


```{r perform clr-transormation in sediment core data}

clr_data_C2 <- clr_transform(XRF_C2, elements_keep_C2, C2_depth)

clr_data_C3 <- clr_transform(XRF_C3, elements_keep_C3, C3_depth)

```


#C2 Analysis

```{r PCA C2}

# --- Subset Data for PCA ---
# Only include the elements you wish to use for the PCA
subset_data_C2 <- clr_data_C2 %>% dplyr::select(-c("Depth", "K", "Cl")) #Taking pout K and Cl as they are probably artifacts from the drying process. 


# --- Perform PCA on clr-transformed data ---
pca_results_C2 <- prcomp(subset_data_C2, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)

# --- Extract PCA Scores ---
scores_C2 <- data.frame(pca_results_C2$x[, 1:2])
colnames(scores_C2) <- c("PC1", "PC2")

# --- Add Depth and Facies Information ---
# Here, we assume you have a depth vector (Depth_cm) from your original data.
# Adjust this accordingly if Depth_cm is stored elsewhere.
scores_C2$Depth <- clr_data_C2$Depth  # Replace 'original_data' with your depth data frame

# Create facies based on depth intervals:
# ZC2A: 107-116 cm, ZC2B: 60-107 cm, ZC2C: 48-60 cm, ZC2D: 30-48 cm, ZC2E: 0-30 cm.
scores_C2$Facies <- cut(scores_C2$Depth,
                     breaks = c(0, 30, 45, 48, 58, 107, 116),
                     labels = c("ZC2A", "ZC2B", "M1", "ZC2C", "ZC2D", "ZC2E"),
                     include.lowest = TRUE, right = TRUE)

# --- Extract and scale Loadings ---
loadings_C2 <- pca_results_C2$rotation[, 1:2] * 
  matrix(pca_results_C2$sdev[1:2], nrow = nrow(pca_results_C2$rotation), ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE)
loadings_C2 <- data.frame(loadings_C2)
colnames(loadings_C2) <- c("PC1", "PC2")
loadings_C2$variable <- rownames(loadings_C2)

####Scale Loadings to the same coordinate system as the scores (using the maximum absolute score). OPTIONAL###
#scale_factor <- max(abs(scores_C2$PC1), abs(scores_C2$PC2))
#loadings_scaled_C2 <- loadings_C2
#loadings_scaled_C2$PC1 <- loadings_scaled_C2$PC1 * scale_factor
#loadings_scaled_C2$PC2 <- loadings_scaled_C2$PC2 * scale_factor

rm(subset_data_C2)

```



```{r Plot C2 Individual Factor Map (OPTIONAL)}

# --- Create the PCA Biplot (Individual Factor Map) ---
pcaC2_biplot_facies <- ggplot() +
  # Plot sample scores, colored by facies
  geom_point(data = scores_C2, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = Facies, text = Depth), alpha = 0.7, size = 2) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "PCA Biplot with Facies for Core C2",
       x = "PC1", y = "PC2", color = "Facies")

# Print the plot
print(pcaC2_biplot_facies)

interactive_plot_PCA_C2 <- ggplotly(pcaC2_biplot_facies, tooltip = c("x", "y", "color", "text"))
```




```{r Plot C2 only the eigenvectors without the scores}

# Create the correlation biplot with fixed axes [-1, 1]
pca_biplot_cor_C2 <- ggplot() +
  geom_segment(data = loadings_C2, 
               aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = PC1, yend = PC2), 
               arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.2, "cm")), 
               color = "black") +
  geom_text_repel(data = loadings_C2, 
                  aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, label = variable), 
                  color = "red", size = 4) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Correlation Biplot from clr-transformed XRF Data in C2", 
       x = "PC1", y = "PC2") +
  xlim(-1, 1) +
  ylim(-1, 1)

print(pca_biplot_cor_C2)


```


```{r plot C2 PCA in facets without loadings}
pcaC2_biplot_facies_facets_C2 <- ggplot() +
  # Plot sample scores, colored by facies
  geom_point(data = scores_C2, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = Facies), alpha = 0.7, size = 2) +
  facet_wrap(~ Facies) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "PCA Biplot Faceted by Facies for Core C2",
       x = "PC1", y = "PC2", color = "Facies")

pcaC2_biplot_facies_facets_C2

```


```{r LDA in C2 (OPTIONAL, DO NOT USE)}

# Assuming clr_data_C2 is your clr-transformed data with a column named "Depth" in mm
# Define facies based on depth intervals in mm:
clr_data_C2$Facies <- cut(clr_data_C2$Depth,
                          breaks = c(0, 30, 48, 60, 107, 116),
                          labels = c("ZC2A", "ZC2B", "ZC2C", "ZC2D", "ZC2E"),
                          include.lowest = TRUE, right = TRUE)

# Check the distribution of facies to ensure each group has observations:
print(table(clr_data_C2$Facies))

# Define the predictor elements for LDA
elements_C2_LDA <- elements_keep_C2

# Prepare the data for LDA by subsetting to the chosen elements and the facies column
lda_data <- clr_data_C2[, c(elements_C2_LDA, "Facies")]

# Perform LDA using the chosen elements to discriminate among facies
lda_model <- lda(Facies ~ ., data = lda_data)
lda_pred <- predict(lda_model)

# Combine the LDA scores with facies information
lda_scores <- data.frame(lda_pred$x)
lda_scores$Facies <- lda_data$Facies

# Plot the first two linear discriminants
lda_plot <- ggplot(lda_scores, aes(x = LD1, y = LD2, color = Facies)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  labs(title = "LDA of Core C2 clr-transformed Data",
       x = "LD1",
       y = "LD2",
       color = "Facies") +
  theme_minimal()

print(lda_plot)



```


```{r Explained variance and Correlation Matrix for C2}
# Explained Variance
explained_variance_C2 <- pca_results_C2$sdev^2 / sum(pca_results_C2$sdev^2)
explained_variance_perc_C2 <- explained_variance_C2 * 100

# Print Explained Variance
print(explained_variance_perc_C2)

# Creating the barplot for Explained Variance
pc_var_C2 <- data.frame(PC = seq(along = explained_variance_perc_C2), Variance = explained_variance_perc_C2)
barplot_C2 <- ggplot(pc_var_C2, aes(x = factor(PC), y = Variance)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "gray") +
    labs(x = "Principal Component", y = "Percentage of Variance Explained (%)") +
    ggtitle("Scree Plot of PCA in C2") +
    theme_minimal()

# Print the barplot
barplot_C2

# Print Loadings Correlation Matrix
cor_loadings_C2 <- pca_results_C2$rotation  # PCA loadings
cor_matrix_C2 <- cor(cor_loadings_C2)
cor_matrix_C2

names(explained_variance_perc_C2) <- colnames(cor_matrix_C2)

```


```{r Save Figures and data for C2}

#Saving PCA plots
ggsave("plots/C2/pca_biplot_cor_C2.svg", pca_biplot_cor_C2, width = 6, height = 6, units = "in", device = "svg")
ggsave("plots/C2/pca_biplot_cor_C2.tiff", pca_biplot_cor_C2, width = 6, height = 6, dpi = 300)
ggsave("plots/C2/pcaC2_biplot_facies_facets_C2.svg", pcaC2_biplot_facies_facets_C2, width = 6, height = 6, units = "in", device = "svg")
ggsave("plots/C2/pcaC2_biplot_facies_facets_C2.tiff", pcaC2_biplot_facies_facets_C2, width = 6, height = 6, dpi = 300)

ggsave("plots/C2/pcaC2_biplot_facies.svg", pcaC2_biplot_facies, width = 6, height = 6, units = "in", device = "svg")


#Save barplot
ggsave("plots/C2/barplot_C2.svg", barplot_C2, width = 6, height = 6, units = "in", device = "svg")


#Save CLR and PCA scores for MATLAB plotting
write.csv(clr_data_C2, file = "outputFiles/C2/clr_data_C2.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(scores_C2, file = "outputFiles/C2/PCA_data_C2.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(explained_variance_perc_C2, file = "outputFiles/C2/C2_Variance_Perc.csv", row.names = FALSE)

#Save correlation Matrix as a CSV file

write.csv(cor_matrix_C2, file = "outputFiles/C2/correlation_matrix_C2.csv", row.names = T)

rm(pca_biplot_cor_C2)
rm(pcaC2_biplot_facies_facets_C2)
rm(cor_matrix_C2)
rm(scores_C2)
rm(XRF_C2)
rm(clr_data_C2)
rm(pca_results_C2)
rm(loadings_C2)
rm(pc_var_C2)
rm(cor_loadings_C2)
rm(barplot_C2)

```


#C3 Analysis


```{r PCA C3}

# --- Subset Data for PCA ---
# Only include the elements you wish to use for the PCA
subset_data_C3 <- clr_data_C3 %>% dplyr::select(-c("Depth", "K", "Cl"))

# --- Perform PCA on clr-transformed data ---
pca_results_C3 <- prcomp(subset_data_C3, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)

# --- Extract PCA Scores ---
scores_C3 <- data.frame(pca_results_C3$x[, 1:2])
colnames(scores_C3) <- c("PC1", "PC2")

# --- Add Depth and Facies Information ---
# Here, we assume you have a depth vector (Depth_cm) from your original data.
# Adjust this accordingly if Depth_cm is stored elsewhere.
scores_C3$Depth <- clr_data_C3$Depth  # Replace 'original_data' with your depth data frame

# Create facies based on depth intervals:
# ZC3A: 55-95 cm, ZC3B: 40-55 cm, ZC3C: 20-40 cm, ZC2D: 0-20 cm.
scores_C3$Facies <- cut(scores_C3$Depth,
                     breaks = c(0, 15, 38, 53.6, 94),
                     labels = c("ZC3A", "ZC3B", "ZC3C", "ZC3D"),
                     include.lowest = TRUE, right = TRUE)

#Eliminate ant NaN values
scores_C3 <- scores_C3[complete.cases(scores_C3), ]


# --- Extract and scale Loadings ---
loadings_C3 <- pca_results_C3$rotation[, 1:2] * 
  matrix(pca_results_C3$sdev[1:2], nrow = nrow(pca_results_C3$rotation), ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE)
loadings_C3 <- data.frame(loadings_C3)
colnames(loadings_C3) <- c("PC1", "PC2")
loadings_C3$variable <- rownames(loadings_C3)

####Scale Loadings to the same coordinate system as the scores (using the maximum absolute score). OPTIONAL###
#scale_factor_C3 <- max(abs(scores_C3$PC1), abs(scores_C3$PC2))
#loadings_scaled_C3 <- loadings_C3
#loadings_scaled_C3$PC1 <- loadings_scaled_C3$PC1 * scale_factor_C3
#loadings_scaled_C3$PC2 <- loadings_scaled_C3$PC2 * scale_factor_C3

rm(subset_data_C3)

```


```{r Plot C3 Individual Factor Map (OPTIONAL)}

# --- Create the PCA Biplot (Individual Factor Map) ---
pcaC3_biplot_facies <- ggplot() +
  # Plot sample scores, colored by facies
  geom_point(data = scores_C3, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = Facies, text = Depth), alpha = 0.7, size = 2) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "PCA Biplot with Facies for Core C3",
       x = "PC1", y = "PC2", color = "Facies")

# Print the plot
print(pcaC3_biplot_facies)

interactive_plot_PCA_C3 <- ggplotly(pcaC3_biplot_facies, tooltip = c("x", "y", "color", "text"))
```

```{r Plot C3 only the eigenvectors without the scores}

# Create the correlation biplot with fixed axes [-1, 1]
pca_biplot_cor_C3 <- ggplot() +
  geom_segment(data = loadings_C3, 
               aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = PC1, yend = PC2), 
               arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.2, "cm")), 
               color = "black") +
  geom_text_repel(data = loadings_C3, 
                  aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, label = variable), 
                  color = "red", size = 4) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Correlation Biplot from clr-transformed XRF Data in C3", 
       x = "PC1", y = "PC2") +
  xlim(-1, 1) +
  ylim(-1, 1)

print(pca_biplot_cor_C3)


```


```{r plot C3 PCA in facets without loadings}
pcaC2_biplot_facies_facets_C3 <- ggplot() +
  # Plot sample scores, colored by facies
  geom_point(data = scores_C3, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = Facies), alpha = 0.7, size = 2) +
  facet_wrap(~ Facies) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "PCA Biplot Faceted by Facies for Core C3",
       x = "PC1", y = "PC2", color = "Facies")

pcaC2_biplot_facies_facets_C3

```

```{r Explained variance and Correlation Matrix for C3}
# Explained Variance
explained_variance_C3 <- pca_results_C3$sdev^2 / sum(pca_results_C3$sdev^2)
explained_variance_perc_C3 <- explained_variance_C3 * 100

# Print Explained Variance
print(explained_variance_perc_C3)

# Creating the barplot for Explained Variance
pc_var_C3 <- data.frame(PC = seq(along = explained_variance_perc_C3), Variance = explained_variance_perc_C3)
barplot_C3 <- ggplot(pc_var_C3, aes(x = factor(PC), y = Variance)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "gray") +
    labs(x = "Principal Component", y = "Percentage of Variance Explained (%)") +
    ggtitle("Scree Plot of PCA in C3") +
    theme_minimal()

# Print the barplot
barplot_C3

# Print Loadings Correlation Matrix
cor_loadings_C3 <- pca_results_C3$rotation  # PCA loadings
cor_matrix_C3 <- cor(cor_loadings_C3)
cor_matrix_C3

names(explained_variance_perc_C3) <- colnames(cor_matrix_C3)
```



```{r Save Figures and data for C3}

#Saving PCA plots
ggsave("plots/C3/pca_biplot_cor_C3.svg", pca_biplot_cor_C3, width = 6, height = 6, units = "in", device = "svg")
ggsave("plots/C3/pca_biplot_cor_C3.tiff", pca_biplot_cor_C3, width = 6, height = 6, dpi = 300)
ggsave("plots/C3/pcaC2_biplot_facies_facets_C3.svg", pcaC2_biplot_facies_facets_C3, width = 6, height = 6, units = "in", device = "svg")
ggsave("plots/C3/pcaC2_biplot_facies_facets_C3.tiff", pcaC2_biplot_facies_facets_C3, width = 6, height = 6, dpi = 300)

ggsave("plots/C3/pcaC3_biplot_facies.svg", pcaC3_biplot_facies, width = 6, height = 6, units = "in", device = "svg")


#Save barplot
ggsave("plots/C3/barplot_C3.svg", barplot_C3, width = 6, height = 6, units = "in", device = "svg")


#Save CLR and PCA scores for MATLAB plotting
write.csv(clr_data_C3, file = "outputFiles/C3/clr_data_C3.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(scores_C3, file = "outputFiles/C3/PCA_data_C3.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(explained_variance_perc_C3, file = "outputFiles/C3/C3_Variance_Perc.csv", row.names = FALSE)

#Save correlation Matrix as a CSV file

write.csv(cor_matrix_C3, file = "outputFiles/C3/correlation_matrix_C3.csv", row.names = T)


```


## C3 analysis by portions. 

```{r Carbonate Sediment PCA}

# --- Subset Data for PCA ---
# Only include the elements you wish to use for the PCA
C3_inorganic_og <- subset(clr_data_C3, Depth >=53.6 & Depth <=94)

C3_inorganic <- C3_inorganic_og %>% select(-c("Depth", "K", "Cl"))

# --- Perform PCA on clr-transformed data ---
pca_results_C3_Peat <- prcomp(C3_inorganic, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)

# --- Extract PCA Scores ---
scores_C3_Peat <- data.frame(pca_results_C3_Peat$x[, 1:2])
colnames(scores_C3_Peat) <- c("PC1", "PC2")

# --- Add Depth and Facies Information ---
# Here, we assume you have a depth vector (Depth_cm) from your original data.
# Adjust this accordingly if Depth_cm is stored elsewhere.
scores_C3_Peat$Depth <- C3_inorganic_og$Depth  # Replace 'original_data' with your depth data frame


# --- Extract and scale Loadings ---
loadings_C3_Peat <- pca_results_C3_Peat$rotation[, 1:2] * 
  matrix(pca_results_C3_Peat$sdev[1:2], nrow = nrow(pca_results_C3_Peat$rotation), ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE)
loadings_C3_Peat <- data.frame(loadings_C3_Peat)
colnames(loadings_C3_Peat) <- c("PC1", "PC2")
loadings_C3_Peat$variable <- rownames(loadings_C3_Peat)

# Create the correlation biplot with fixed axes [-1, 1]
pca_biplot_cor_C3_Peat <- ggplot() +
  geom_segment(data = loadings_C3_Peat, 
               aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = PC1, yend = PC2), 
               arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.2, "cm")), 
               color = "black") +
  geom_text_repel(data = loadings_C3_Peat, 
                  aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, label = variable), 
                  color = "red", size = 4) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Correlation Biplot from clr-transformed XRF Data in C3- Peat", 
       x = "PC1", y = "PC2") +
  xlim(-1, 1) +
  ylim(-1, 1)

print(pca_biplot_cor_C3_Peat)


```

```{r Peat PCA}

# --- Subset Data for PCA ---
# Only include the elements you wish to use for the PCA
C3_Peat <- subset(clr_data_C3, Depth >=0 & Depth <=53.5)

C3_Peat <- C3_Peat %>% select(-c("Depth", "K", "Cl"))

# --- Perform PCA on clr-transformed data ---
pca_results_C3_Peat <- prcomp(C3_Peat, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)

# --- Extract PCA Scores ---
scores_C3_Peat <- data.frame(pca_results_C3_Peat$x[, 1:2])
colnames(scores_C3_Peat) <- c("PC1", "PC2")

# --- Add Depth and Facies Information ---
# Here, we assume you have a depth vector (Depth_cm) from your original data.
# Adjust this accordingly if Depth_cm is stored elsewhere.
scores_C3_Peat$Depth <- C3_Peat$Depth  # Replace 'original_data' with your depth data frame

# Create facies based on depth intervals:
# ZC3A: 55-95 cm, ZC3B: 40-55 cm, ZC3C: 20-40 cm, ZC2D: 0-20 cm.
scores_C3_Peat$Facies <- cut(scores_C3_Peat$Depth,
                     breaks = c(0, 20, 40, 53),
                     labels = c("ZC3A", "ZC3B", "ZC3C"),
                     include.lowest = TRUE, right = TRUE)

#Eliminate ant NaN values
scores_C3_Peat <- scores_C3_Peat[complete.cases(scores_C3_Peat), ]


# --- Extract and scale Loadings ---
loadings_C3_Inor <- pca_results_C3_Peat$rotation[, 1:2] * 
  matrix(pca_results_C3_Peat$sdev[1:2], nrow = nrow(pca_results_C3_Peat$rotation), ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE)
loadings_C3_Inor <- data.frame(loadings_C3_Inor)
colnames(loadings_C3_Inor) <- c("PC1", "PC2")
loadings_C3_Inor$variable <- rownames(loadings_C3_Inor)

# Create the correlation biplot with fixed axes [-1, 1]
pca_biplot_cor_C3_Carb <- ggplot() +
  geom_segment(data = loadings_C3_Inor, 
               aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = PC1, yend = PC2), 
               arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.2, "cm")), 
               color = "black") +
  geom_text_repel(data = loadings_C3_Inor, 
                  aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, label = variable), 
                  color = "red", size = 4) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Correlation Biplot from clr-transformed XRF Data in C3", 
       x = "PC1", y = "PC2") +
  xlim(-1, 1) +
  ylim(-1, 1)

print(pca_biplot_cor_C3_Carb)

pcaC2_biplot_facies_facets_C3_Inor <- ggplot() +
  # Plot sample scores, colored by facies
  geom_point(data = scores_C3_Peat, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = Facies), alpha = 0.7, size = 2) +
  facet_wrap(~ Facies) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "PCA Biplot Faceted by Facies for Core C3",
       x = "PC1", y = "PC2", color = "Facies")

pcaC2_biplot_facies_facets_C3_Inor

# --- Create the PCA Biplot (Individual Factor Map) ---
pcaC3_biplot_facies_Inor <- ggplot() +
  # Plot sample scores, colored by facies
  geom_point(data = scores_C3_Peat, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = Facies, text = Depth), alpha = 0.7, size = 2) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "PCA Biplot with Facies for Core C3",
       x = "PC1", y = "PC2", color = "Facies")

# Print the plot
print(pcaC3_biplot_facies_Inor)

interactive_plot_PCA_C3_Inor <- ggplotly(pcaC3_biplot_facies_Inor, tooltip = c("x", "y", "color", "text"))
```
